name: Ixonia Bank
description_short: Locally-owned Wisconsin community bank offering personal and business banking with 105+ years of history. Serves southeastern Wisconsin with multiple branch locations.
description_long:
Ixonia Bank is a community bank based in Wisconsin that has operated for over 105 years, establishing itself as a locally-owned and operated financial institution. The bank explicitly positions itself as a 'boutique commercial and private bank passionately committed to building authentic relationships,' with a focus on serving both individuals and hundreds of businesses across southeastern Wisconsin.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of banking products including personal checking and savings accounts, CDs and IRAs, health savings accounts, mortgage loans, car loans, consumer loans, and credit cards. On the commercial side, they provide business checking and savings accounts, commercial loans and lines of credit, business credit cards, employee retirement plans, treasury management, merchant services, and remote deposit capture. Their digital offerings include online banking, mobile banking, online bill pay, and card management through mobile wallet functionality.

Ixonia Bank distinguishes itself through its local ownership structure and long operational history in the community. Recent recognition as a 2025 Wisconsin SBA Volume Lender Award recipient indicates their commitment to small business lending. They maintain seven branch locations across southeastern Wisconsin (Ixonia, Dousman, Milwaukee, two Oconomowoc locations, and two Watertown locations), providing physical accessibility alongside their digital banking infrastructure. The bank emphasizes security and fraud prevention as core priorities.

As a traditional community bank, Ixonia Bank is best suited for customers seeking personalized service and local decision-making rather than digital-first banking experiences. Their strength lies in relationship-based banking and commercial lending support, though specific rate competitiveness and fee structures are not detailed on their public website. Like most regional banks, they may have more limited product innovation compared to larger national institutions or specialized fintech lenders.
services: Commercial loans and lines of credit
pros: 105+ years of operational history and local ownership in Wisconsin
Multiple branch locations across southeastern Wisconsin for in-person banking
Comprehensive product suite for both personal and business customers
2025 Wisconsin SBA Volume Lender Award recognition demonstrates strong small business lending commitment
Digital banking capabilities including mobile banking, online bill pay, and mobile wallet
Treasury management and merchant services for business customers
Emphasis on fraud prevention and account security as stated priority
cons: Limited geographic reach—only serves southeastern Wisconsin, not available nationwide
No specific interest rates or fee information disclosed on website, requiring direct contact
Smaller institution may have fewer product variations or competitive rates compared to national banks
No mention of special loan products (FHA, VA, reverse mortgages) despite mortgage offerings
Limited online account opening details—process not fully transparent on website
best_for: Small business owners in southeastern Wisconsin seeking relationship-based commercial banking
Wisconsin residents wanting local decision-making and community reinvestment
Customers who value in-person branch access and personalized service
diagnosis: Ixonia Bank is best for Wisconsin residents and southeastern Wisconsin businesses seeking community-based banking with local ownership and relationship-focused service. The primary caveat is limited geographic availability—this bank only serves Wisconsin, making it unsuitable for customers outside this region or those requiring national banking infrastructure.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
